name: Geʽez (Classical Ethiopic)
notes: STRUCTURAL ANCESTRY NODE (not a modelled G2P target). Geʽez is Classical Ethiopic, the liturgical South-Semitic language of Ethiopia and the literary ancestor / prestige source of the modern Ethiopian Semitic languages Amharic and Tigrinya. It exists here only so those specs have a node to resolve to; the grapheme set is a placeholder and no phonology is claimed (trk.json stub pattern).

sources.author: Gragg, G.
sources.title: Geʽez (in: The Ancient Languages of Mesopotamia, Egypt and Aksum)
sources.notes: Reference for Classical Ethiopic (ancestry node only).
